text.title: competitive-positioning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for competitive-positioning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: "Better" positioning: competitive claims of faster, easier, simpler without a stated dimension and number.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Comparative-without-dimension claims cannot be proven, are easily matched by competitors, and signal that the team has not found real differentiation. "5x faster at data export than Salesforce" is allowed; "faster" alone is not.

failure-mode.description: Static positioning: canvas written once and never revisited as market changes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Version the canvas in git with a date stamp. Quarterly diff reveals positioning drift before customers notice. Markets shift; positioning must track.

failure-mode.description: Best-fit-customer defined as "ambitious teams who want to ship faster."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enforce a persona schema: role, company size, trigger event, current alternative, willingness-to-pay. Without schema enforcement, agents produce meaningless audience descriptions.

failure-mode.description: Comparative language by default: LLMs produce "better/faster/easier" even when prompted for differentiation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicit prompt rule plus positioning-lint.sh pre-commit hook catches it. Comparative without dimension+number is the single most common positioning quality failure from LLM agents.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated competitor positioning from training data instead of current homepages.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pass live homepage HTML/text into the prompt. Never let the agent rely on memory for competitor claims — they are updated constantly and the agent's training is stale.

failure-mode.description: Ladder collapse: so-what laddering produces three levels that paraphrase the same idea.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force each level to use a different noun class (feature benefit → business outcome → emotional/strategic outcome) and a measurable at the terminal level.

failure-mode.description: Category-creation inflation: every product becomes "the first AI-native X."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require a citation that the proposed category does not yet exist — G2 search returning zero results, or no Wikipedia entry for the category name. Fiction categories harm credibility.

failure-mode.description: Proof points filled with "trusted by leaders" marketing copy.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every proof point must be a citable URL, customer quote, or measurable outcome. Marketing copy is not evidence and fails the agent's own lint rules.
